questions.text: A substance containing two or more elements chemically combined in a fixed ratio is called:
questions.explanation: A compound is a pure substance made of two or more elements chemically combined in a fixed proportion by mass.

questions.text: Which of the following is a homogeneous mixture?
questions.explanation: Homogeneous mixtures have uniform composition throughout. Salt solution and air are homogeneous.

questions.text: The Tyndall effect is observed in colloids.
questions.explanation: The Tyndall effect (scattering of light) is observed in colloidal solutions due to their particle size.

questions.text: The technique used to separate butter from curd is ________.
questions.explanation: Centrifugation separates particles based on density. It's used to separate butter from curd/cream from milk.
